title: Galearia wallichii
intro: Galearia wallichii, also known as Indian gentian, is a type of flowering plant in the gentian family that is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Galearia wallichii include compounds such as gentiopicrin and gentianine, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Galearia wallichii is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is less commonly used in modern Western herbalism compared to other gentian species.
taxonomy: Galearia wallichii is a species of orchid. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae. The genus Galearia is a part of the subfamily Orchidoideae.
